_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=72 with 8 verified claims. ILO source corrected to MOF (Ministry of Finance) for the 2.5M figure. Employment Act amendment date specified as 1 January 2023 effective. Source-country distribution updated to MOF end-August 2024 figures (Bangladesh 37.8% / Indonesia 23.7% / Nepal 16.7%); Myanmar dropped pending verification. 'Migrant workers anchor the export base' framing softened to 'foreign workers are officially allowed in' per Stage 3 OVERCLAIM flag. View card 'Tier 1 requires sectoral prosecutions' (INCORRECT — TVPA does not specify sector) replaced with 'TIP urges investigating and prosecuting credible allegations'.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'ILO data placed registered migrant workers at ~2.5 million' → 'MOF data' (the 2.5M figure is verified but the source is the Ministry of Finance, not ILO).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Employment Act amendment effective date clarified: enacted 2022, took effect 1 January 2023.
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED_DETAIL — 'Enforcement targets individual workplaces, not systemic chains' framing dropped; replaced with TIP-Report-anchored 'credible allegations went unaddressed'.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — 'Migrant workers anchor palm oil, electronics... the export base' replaced with verified MOF-anchored 'Foreign workers are officially allowed in manufacturing, construction, agriculture, plantations and services'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — source-country distribution narrowed to MOF-verified Bangladesh 37.8% / Indonesia 23.7% / Nepal 16.7%; Myanmar removed pending separate verification.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe now references actual Tier 3 → Tier 2 trajectory (Malaysia was Tier 3 in 2014) rather than implying static multi-year warning.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — View card 'Tier 1 requires sectoral prosecutions in palm oil and gloves' replaced with 'TIP urges investigating and prosecuting credible allegations'. TVPA does not specify sectoral prosecutions; Tier 1 requires full TVPA minimum-standards compliance.

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): Tier 2 status 2025 TIP; palm-oil and gloves forced-labour concerns; weak prosecution; Employment Act amendments core fact; forced-labour indicators across sectors; 'no labour-trafficking prosecutions in palm oil or glove manufacturing'.
